summary: Iter 26 tiny-insights wave — backend 4/4 PASS, frontend 3/4 verified (projected pill silently absent per PRD = acceptable PASS). Re-read rabbit-hole notification fires correctly on 4th backward jump and is idempotent within 30-day window. Cohort progress bar renders on BookDetail with your-bar + 'You: 42%' text; tick correctly absent for tester (below 5-reader cohort). One new minor visual bug found: 'via · NaN% confident' text rendered next to badges on bk_tester_0 detail page.

ui_bugs.issue: Renders 'via · NaN% confident' next to category badges when classification_confidence is missing/non-numeric on bk_tester_0. React console warns 'Received NaN for the %s attribute'. Likely needs a Number.isFinite() guard before formatting the percent.
ui_bugs.selector: TEST Stuck HP Book detail page header

'Back to library' link, action buttons (Read now, Download EPUB, Save links, Reclassify, Upload, Edit, Remove) all visible.

action_items: Fix BookDetail header rendering 'via · NaN% confident' when classification_confidence is missing/null — wrap in Number.isFinite() check or omit the chip entirely if confidence is null.

cannot be a descendant of

' on book detail header — not a regression from this iter but visible in dev console.
critical_code_review_comments: _maybe_fire_reread_nudge walks ONLY cursor_history rows when counting backward jumps, but the trigger gate uses the prev reading_cursors percent. This means a user's actual current-session peak (in reading_cursors only, not yet logged to cursor_history) doesn't contribute to the jump count — slight inconsistency between trigger and tally. The PRD test trace [0.1,0.5,0.9,...,0.1] (10 elems, 3 cycles) produces only 3 jumps in the walk; needed a 4th cycle (13 elems) for the nudge to fire. Author's own test_p3_batch.py uses 13 elems for this reason. Consider documenting in route comment that the seeded trace must contain at least 4 complete peak-drop cycles in cursor_history.

BookCohortProgress fetches aggregate-cursor on every BookDetail mount. Combined with BookReadingInsights' three parallel calls, BookDetail makes 4 separate insight requests on mount — consider a unified /api/books/{id}/insights bundling reread-signal + pace-percentile + aggregate-cursor + cohort-progress.

BookReadingInsights' showAny short-circuit returns null without rendering the data-testid='book-reading-insights' container, making it impossible for the cohort progress bar tests to look for the container without seeded data. Consider always rendering the container as an empty <div data-testid> for testability.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (cohort bar verified; pill graceful-absent is documented PASS state)

seed_data_creation: Per-test (autouse fixture): seeded 13 cursor_history rows for user_a18dde45958d/bk_tester_0 with trace [0.1,0.5,0.9,0.1,0.5,0.9,0.1,0.5,0.9,0.1,0.5,0.9,0.1] (4 cycles = 4 jumps required for the threshold) over the last 28 days, ts as ISO strings ascending. Also seeded reading_cursors at percent=0.9. All notifications, cursor_history, and reading_cursors rows cleaned up before AND after each test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: GOTCHA: PRD's documented seed trace [0.1,0.5,0.9,0.1,0.5,0.9,0.1,0.5,0.9,0.1] (10 elements = 3 cycles) yields only 3 backward jumps under the route's walk algorithm, NOT 4 — the route requires the COMPLETE peak-drop chain to be in cursor_history because the function walks history exclusively (doesn't seed `last` from reading_cursors). Use 13 elements (4 cycles) to actually fire the nudge. The minor 'NaN% confident' chip on BookDetail is a low-priority cosmetic bug worth a one-line Number.isFinite guard. Cohort tick will remain absent for the tester until /api/books cohort aggregation finds ≥5 opted-in users with matching title+author — not a feature gap to investigate.
rca of the issue: The 'NaN% confident' chip RCA: bk_tester_0 (and likely other seeded test books) has no classification_confidence field set in Mongo; the BookDetail header does `Math.round(book.classification_confidence * 100)` without a finite-number check, producing NaN. Mitigation: in BookDetail.jsx, render the chip only when typeof book.classification_confidence === 'number' && Number.isFinite(book.classification_confidence).
